signals.reasoning: BTC is still chopping in a fragile post-flush range while the board has moved back to full VETO. That combination makes a fresh directional call lower quality than staying flat.
ETH has stabilized but not cleanly resolved. With macro still RED and the event brake back at full strength, there is not enough edge for a new position.
SOL still has some squeeze fuel and a better intraday bounce than the majors, but the active veto keeps this as a watch name rather than a responsible fresh long.
STABLE has strong negative-funding squeeze mechanics on paper, but the move is already extended and highly exposed to headline whipsaw. Under full veto, that is not a chaseable long.
AAVE has decent liquidity and real rebound strength, but the move is already mature enough that the reward-to-risk is poor under a live geopolitical veto. It stays watch-only.

LIT is one of the few names still showing active bearish structure, with price weak and open interest still building on the short side. Confidence stays reduced because the board is vetoed, but if anything survives as a short lean, this is the cleanest one.
DASH is bouncing with OI support, which makes the chart too mixed for a fresh short, but the event regime also keeps it from being a confident long. Best left alone.
DOT has heavy negative funding, but the chart is still damaged and the move lower is already fairly mature. Under VETO, it does not clear the bar for a squeeze-long upgrade.
GOAT is too deep into flush territory for a fresh short and too unstable for a fresh long. There is no good entry here.
0G remains the strongest raw squeeze setup on the board, but it is still extended, thin, and vulnerable to violent reversal. Full veto makes this a watchlist name, not an execution name.
BLUR has already done most of its downside work and now looks more like post-flush stabilization than a clean continuation short. It stays off the active list.
